# Std.Fs

<!-- generated by `flx docs build`; do not edit -->

*Generated from the `doc` declarations in `Fs.flx` by `flx docs build`. Examples are executed by `flx docs check`.*

Narrow text-file helpers for CLI tools.

These are intentionally text APIs. Flex strings are NUL-terminated, so
`read_text` rejects files containing byte 0 instead of silently truncating
them. Use a `List<I64>` byte buffer for binary data.

*since 0.0.1 · status: implemented*

## read_text

```flx
fn read_text(path: String) -> Result<String, String> uses { Fs }
```

Read a whole text file, or return Err(reason).

The returned string preserves non-UTF-8 bytes losslessly with the same byte
representation used by string literals and argv. Files containing NUL bytes
return `Err`, because String cannot carry byte 0.

**Sketch: read a config file** — illustration only, not checked:

```flx
match read_text("config.txt") {
  Ok(text) => { text }
  Err(reason) => { reason }
}
```

*since 0.0.1 · status: implemented*

See also: `Std.Str`

## write_text

```flx
fn write_text(path: String, contents: String) -> Result<Unit, String> uses { Fs }
```

Write a whole string to a file, replacing existing contents.

**Sketch: write a todo file** — illustration only, not checked:

```flx
match write_text("todo.txt", "buy milk\n") {
  Ok(_) => {}
  Err(reason) => { reason }
}
```

*since 0.0.1 · status: implemented*
